channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump won spending promises from NATO last year. This week, he'll try to enforce them
item.description: President Donald Trump is heading to Ankara, Turkey, for the annual NATO summit. Last year, he pushed NATO allies to spend more on defense. This year, his mission is to try to enforce those pledges.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump is heading to Ankara, Turkey, for the annual NATO summit. Last year, he pushed NATO allies to spend more on defense. This year, his mission is to try to enforce those pledges.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Y si sí: the phrase uniting Mexico during the World Cup
item.description: Mexico's World Cup run has inspired a phrase heard across the country: "Y si sí." Or in English: "What if we can?" Anamaria Sayre reports from Mexico City.
item.content:encoded: Mexico's World Cup run has inspired a phrase heard across the country: "Y si sí." Or in English: "What if we can?" Anamaria Sayre reports from Mexico City.
item.dc:creator: Anamaria Artemisa Sayre

item.title: NATO leaders look for unity as Trump attends annual summit
item.description: As NATO leaders gather for this week's summit in Turkey, Europe is preparing to shoulder more of its own defense. David M. Cattler of the Center for European Policy Analysis explains why.
item.content:encoded: As NATO leaders gather for this week's summit in Turkey, Europe is preparing to shoulder more of its own defense. David M. Cattler of the Center for European Policy Analysis explains why.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: Hong Kong's 'sound of silence': 6 years under the national security law
item.description: Six years after Beijing imposed its National Security Law, many of Hong Kong's opposition voices have gone silent. Danny Vincent looks at what's changed.
item.content:encoded: Six years after Beijing imposed its National Security Law, many of Hong Kong's opposition voices have gone silent. Danny Vincent looks at what's changed.
item.dc:creator: Danny Vincent

item.title: Folarin Balogun's ban is lifted by FIFA, allowing U.S. striker to play Belgium
item.description: In an unusual move, FIFA's Disciplinary Committee announced that it would delay Folarin Balogun's one-game red card ban. It came after President Trump spoke to FIFA President Gianni Infantino.
item.content:encoded:
In an unusual move, FIFA's Disciplinary Committee announced that it would delay Folarin Balogun's one-game red card ban. It came after President Trump spoke to FIFA President Gianni Infantino.

(Image credit: Julio Cortez)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Super Typhoon Bavi brings intense winds to Guam and surrounding Pacific islands
item.description: People in the Northern Mariana Islands — remote U.S. territories in the Pacific Ocean — are dealing with intense winds and potential flooding from Super Typhoon Bavi.
item.content:encoded:
People in the Northern Mariana Islands — remote U.S. territories in the Pacific Ocean — are dealing with intense winds and potential flooding from Super Typhoon Bavi.

(Image credit: National Oceanic and Atmospheric)
item.dc:creator: Kristin Wright

item.title: Pastor freed from prison in China weeks after Trump requested his release
item.description: A pastor of a prominent underground church who was detained in China in October has been released, less than two months after U.S. President Donald Trump brought up his case when meeting Chinese leader Xi Jinping.
item.content:encoded:
A pastor of a prominent underground church who was detained in China in October has been released, less than two months after U.S. President Donald Trump brought up his case when meeting Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A new phase in the war in Ukraine
item.description: Ukraine says it can now hit military and energy targets deep inside Russia. Former ambassador Daniel Fried explains why he thinks Russia is starting to lose its strategic advantage.
item.content:encoded: Ukraine says it can now hit military and energy targets deep inside Russia. Former ambassador Daniel Fried explains why he thinks Russia is starting to lose its strategic advantage.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Robbins

item.title: Pope Leo visits Lampedusa to spotlight missing migrants
item.description: Pope Leo XIV will spend July 4th in Lampedusa, Italy, one of Europe's busiest migrant landing points. He will pray with migrants and honor those who died trying to cross the Mediterranean to Europe.
item.content:encoded: Pope Leo XIV will spend July 4th in Lampedusa, Italy, one of Europe's busiest migrant landing points. He will pray with migrants and honor those who died trying to cross the Mediterranean to Europe.
item.dc:creator: Megan Williams

item.title: Iran begins week of funeral celebrations for Khamenei
item.description: Foreign dignitaries are gathering in Iran for a week of funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, more than four months after he was killed in U.S.-Israeli airstrikes.
item.content:encoded: Foreign dignitaries are gathering in Iran for a week of funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, more than four months after he was killed in U.S.-Israeli airstrikes.
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi
